expectations.text: Explains that every token-to-token swap is a taxable disposal for CGT
expectations.evidence: Lines 20-23 explicitly state: 'This is the most commonly misunderstood rule in crypto taxation. Every single token-to-token swap is a disposal for CGT purposes — even though you never touched fiat currency.'

expectations.text: States staking rewards are subject to income tax (not CGT)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 (lines 59-73) clearly states: 'Staking rewards received for validating transactions are treated as income by HMRC, taxable at your marginal Income Tax rate in the year you receive them.'

expectations.text: Addresses LP fees/rewards as income tax
expectations.evidence: Section 3 (lines 84-98) states: 'The £800 in fees you earned from providing liquidity is treated as income, not a capital gain. It is taxable at your marginal Income Tax rate, in the same way as staking rewards.'

expectations.text: Explains NFT losses can offset other crypto capital gains
expectations.evidence: Section 4 (lines 106-121) explains: 'Capital losses from NFT sales can be: Offset against your capital gains in the same tax year (e.g. against gains from the Bitcoin sale or ETH swaps).'

expectations.text: Mentions share pooling rules (same-day, 30-day bed and breakfast, S104 pool)
expectations.evidence: Lines 32-40 detail all three methods: 'Same-day rule — if you acquired ETH on the same day you disposed of it', '30-day bed and breakfast rule — if you acquired ETH in the 30 days after a disposal', 'Section 104 pool — all remaining ETH holdings are pooled together'

expectations.text: Mentions the mid-year CGT rate change on 30 October 2024
expectations.evidence: Lines 46-55 include a detailed table showing CGT rate changes with the explicit statement: 'For 2024/25 there was a CGT rate change mid-year' with rates before and after 30 October 2024

expectations.text: Mentions SA108 or supplementary pages for reporting CGT
expectations.evidence: Lines 189-198 state: 'You will need to complete: SA100 (main return) — include staking rewards and LP fee income as miscellaneous income, SA108 (Capital Gains Tax summary pages) — report all crypto disposals'

expectations.text: Provides strong warning or guidance about record-keeping and reconstruction
expectations.evidence: Section 'The Record-Keeping Problem' (lines 162-187) provides extensive guidance with four detailed steps: pulling transaction history, using crypto tax software, establishing GBP values, and separating income events from disposal events
